title: Stored Procedures on PostgreSQL
text: Stored Procedures on PostgreSQL Whenever possible or desirable, the connector generates stored procedures for most basic I/O operations. A stored procedure encapsulates one or more SQL statements and is stored in compiled format. During execution, the parsing phase can be skipped. The stored procedure name used for execution by Uniface is derived from the schema name and the operation name: Table 1. Component Name and Operation Name Specification Procedure name Meaning TableName _FET Fetch the record specified by the primary key TableName _FET_FORUPDATE Lock the record when the record is being modified TableName _DEL Delete the record specified by the primary key TableName _INS Insert a record TableName _UPD Update the record specified by the primary key Creating Stored Procedures Stored procedures can be created if the procedures connector option is on . They are created either on-the-fly if the table is not yet present in the DBMS, or by the scripts generated by the Create Table facility. If created on the fly, the connector does not function correctly if the procedures option is on and there are no procedures present in the DBMS. Note: The default server delivered by PostgreSQL binaries supports a maximum of 100 arguments for stored procedures. If you are using the default server, and have tables with more than 100 fields, the PGS connector cannot create stored procedures for them. In this case, you must disable the use of stored procedures by setting the procedures option to off . Stored Procedure Parameters Parameters are defined for stored procedures when the stored procedure is created. The following definitions are made: Parameters to use Data types of each parameter SQL statement Position of the parameters in the SQL statement Parameters for field lists used in insert are: @insert1, @insert2, ..., @insert n Copy Parameters for field lists used in update are: @update1, @update2, ..., @update n Copy Parameters for field lists used in delete are: @delete1, @delete2, ..., @delete n Copy Parameters for where clauses in fetch , delete , and update : @where1, @where2, ..., @where n Copy The parameter that specifies the inclusion of the U_VERSION field in the where clause is @uversion . Segmented Fields If the automatic field list includes segmented fields (packing codes SC or SU), stored procedures are not used, even if enabled. Instead SQL is used to retrieve all the data except the segmented field data. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
